ops.title: wire server: v2 persistent sessions, dispatch classes, trust floor

ops.body:
# v2 session architecture (7623022; the v1 LF-framed dispatch this note first documented is historical — see bottom)

Server serves persistent multiplexed exact-v2 sessions (length-prefixed frames, wire/frame.go) on a caller-owned listener it never binds. Per accepted connection: capture Peer once (PeerFromConn immediately after accept) → trust check → mandatory Hello/HelloAck handshake bounded by HandshakeTimeout (strict: FrameHello with ID 0, Sequence 0, FlagEnd, empty op+tenant; exact ProtocolVersion; non-empty build) → session. MaxSessions caps accepted-plus-handshaking connections. Each session runs one readLoop plus one writer goroutine draining a bounded outbound channel (OutboundQueue); Codec reads are single-goroutine, writes serialized and safe from any goroutine.

Two handler classes (v1's exclusive class and tenant-gate serialization did NOT survive; tenant now rides the request envelope only): **control runs inline on the session's read path** — a blocking control handler stalls its own session, never the accept loop; **concurrent** dispatches to a shared bounded pool (Workers slots + Backlog queue), and past capacity the request is Rejected — a proven non-dispatch, safe to replay. Duplicate request IDs are refused per session (seen set + watermark → ErrDuplicateID); stream chunks are sequence-checked (ErrStreamOrder); InboundQueue caps active request IDs per session and StreamQueue caps unread inbound chunks per request. Request contexts cancel on a cancel frame, disconnect, server shutdown, or the earlier Ladder deadline. RegisterLifecycle handlers bypass ordinary admission so shutdown cannot deadlock its own acknowledgement. Serve's admit hook runs once per request; its returned done runs after the terminal response has been written.

# Trust floor (invariant unchanged from v1)

The same-effective-UID floor runs on every accepted connection, and a non-nil Trust callback augments the floor, never replaces it (ErrUntrustedPeer). Timing moved: v1 checked trust after the first frame read so a denial could deliver a rejection; v2 checks BEFORE the handshake reads any frame — a rejected peer gets no session and no reply.

# Shutdown

CloseIntake stops new connections and new ordinary requests; accepted sessions stay alive so admitted work and lifecycle acknowledgements can settle. v1's watchDisconnect short-read-deadline poke is gone: the persistent readLoop observes EOF/reset directly (isDisconnect) and tears down the session's active requests.

# Historical (v1, replaced in 7623022)

LF-framed JSON, one request per connection; three classes (control/concurrent/exclusive) plus per-tenant capacity-1 gates taken on the connection goroutine; watchDisconnect poked the read side under a short deadline while the handler ran; requestGrace/writeGrace bounded the pre-frame read and the response write. Retained here only as design rationale for why v2 folded these into the session read loop.
